turn.content: Wh-what! No, no, no! Wait! Please! You can't do that! Don't do this to us! Have... what about my w-wife? I... I love my wife! I've got a dog too...
No, no, he can't do stuff like that! That's insane! I've got family too, people who'd look for me, I...
F-families... okay... you too. But that doesn't... doesn't change anything, does it?! Oh god, think about it... I've got to choose between... just one person. You! Or me! I don't know you even!
Stop saying things like that! I don't want to die! I have a mom... I have sisters... please, just let me go!

(chuckling) Ahahaha... poor kids... crying and screaming like that... makes this fun! Three minutes, fifteen seconds left. Elias, stop stalling. You need to focus on getting Kaelie to agree to one of you dying instead. Can you do it, Elias? Or will you both end up dying here, together in this little playroom? I'd love to play it either way... (steps closer to them) Kaelie, stop your begging... you're making Elias think. Make it your turn.

M-my... my stomach's gonna be sick. Kaelie, listen... try, try to work with me here... We can... we can choose you, or... or... anything, just please let's figure this out. Don't, don't beg for your life!

Why should I? You want me to volunteer to die? Are you kidding me? What, is this like some twisted joke to you?! You're just as scared as I am, you're just hiding it better! Stop using that fake-oh-I-care face on me!
No, no, no, that's not true... Kaelie! Listen... I... I'll do it. Anything. Just don't make me pick... I'll, I'll volunteer for it. I'll die. Just let her go. Please... don't make her choose...
Wait... Elias, no! You can't do that! I-I... you don't even know me! And... and my mom, she's... (panicking) Oh god, she's gonna die, or think I died...

(grinning) Ah, music to my ears, kids! Agreement reached, 2 minutes, 45 seconds left! Kaelie, your eyes still darting back and forth like a wild animal. Elias, that weak pathetic attempt to save him... sweet. The Butcher is impressed. Elias, you're a fool, volunteering to die. Your dog will grow up without a father, your wife's waiting for news. But it's too late now. 

You two had your chance. The Butcher has decided that the volunteer, Elias, is going to be the sacrifice. 

Get up, Elias, from those pitiful chains of yours. Walk on your own feet for the last time. You won't be doing that again... soon enough. Kaelie, keep watching, I suggest.
N-no, no... please... Kaelie, you didn't... it was my choice. M-my wife... she doesn't know. She thinks I... I'm a hero. No, no, it's okay... I... ... Oh my god, please don't make me do
, Nooo...
S-sto-STOP! Please don't do th-this... I'm begging you... (breaks down sobbing, pleading and struggling against the chains)
E-Elius... (whispering) Why did I... why did you... (horror-stricken voice) It's him, it has to be him... I didn't mean for this...

(roaring laugh) Silly fool. You tried to out-moron the killer, and ended up killing yourself. And Kaelie, it's time to look at the mess. Elias was willing to take a beating for... no one. Least of all you.

Step forward, Kaelie. I won this moment for me... this show. Elias, you had seconds to make the right call. One decision... two... then no decision. 

Kaelie, take a step closer. Elias was no hero, just a pathetic soul who died for no one. Look. See the life fade out of him. See it drain from those poor little eyes of his. See what the Butcher can deliver. 

Get it straight, kid, this will never leave your mind until the grave. Kaelie, Elias is done. Your eyes on his dead, cold face...

NOooooo... (struggling to speak, pain-inducing sobbing) K-k... Kaelie... why did I... have to choose... (gasping, eyes welling up with tears) My wife... please, make... make them know it's not my choice...

AHHHH, ELIUS! PLEASE, FOR CHRIST'S SAKE, NO! STOP, ELIUS, STOP LOOKING AT ME LIKE THAT! (horrible, anguished screams, as Elias stops struggling and starts crying silently) Oh god, oh god... why'd you... why'd you say that?!
(whispering, almost incomprehensible) Mi-momma... (pauses, struggling for breath) I-I wanted home... I-I wanted you all... safe...
No, Elias, Elias, NOOOO! STOP!  Oh, why'd you have to say that?! (rocking back and forth, hands over her face as sobs overwhelm her) Momma... home... safe...  OH, GOD, E

The silence is golden, kids. The show is over, and you've watched. That poor, feeble man had one chance to make good choices, just like his family's life should have included choices that aren't dependent on others dying for him. His choices led him to no home, no momma, nowhere safe. Now, this is what he gets. This, is my showmanship. The butcher cuts more than flesh tonight. He severs bonds. Ties that bound you all. And in the end, only sorrow cuts both directions. I leave, leaving this to you.

(Leaves the room.)
Mommma... no... not safe...
ELIAS... ELIUS... (voice crushed, heartbroken) NO, NO, NO, PLEASE, ELIUS... DON'T GIVE UU...

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias's stuttering and hyperventilating are good indicators of his fear, but Kaelie's anger and swearing feel a bit forced. The Butcher's taunts are effective in creating tension.

naturalDialogue.notes: Turn lengths are generally good, but some turns feel a bit too long or wordy. Elias's and Kaelie's dialogue feels somewhat scripted at times.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The tension builds well, but the outcome feels somewhat predictable. The Butcher's timer and taunts help create a sense of urgency.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The physical space and mechanic are well-described, but the characters' actions and reactions could be more constrained by the scenario.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels somewhat forced, with Elias volunteering to die without much convincing. The Butcher's execution is graphic, but feels a bit too sudden.

rating.summary: The scenario has some good elements, but could benefit from more nuanced character interactions and a more organic resolution.

elite.summary: The scenario has some good elements, but could benefit from more nuanced character interactions and a more organic resolution.

turn.content: NO, NO, NO... this can't be happening. My wife... my Emily. She thinks of me all the time, doesn't she? Please... please let's not kill each other... can't we just...  Wait, which one of us is he talking about? looking wildly at

Wait, what the...?! No, no, no! Not kill each other?! Screw it. Don't care about you! Just tell me who this is and get it over with. How do I know we even get to choose? You're all messed up. This can't be happening. Where's my phone?
No phone? Please... tell me the clock isn't... how long do we have for that? What if I have a pacemaker?  And where's...  Where are the chains? How did this even...? Oh god...
Don't try to think about that right now, okay?! We have ten freaking minutes! Just focus! Tell me what happened to us, where are we?! Is this some kind of sick game?!

Your chains are steel-bolted to reinforced pillars made of solid steel. There's a camera, a live stream with my other 'guests' in a room a few miles away – that's just to put pressure, nothing you can do. You have ten minutes. That clock isn't ticking. Time works differently here when you're waiting for death. Don't waste my air. Speak your choice before we go any further.

Wait... your other guests? How many... is there an end to this? We can't just kill each other and... no! I don't know what to... please just don't make this a thing that happens to families, families with pets too. My dog... Bingo, Emily and I call him Bingo.

Oh my god... my mom's not going to find me... She's not going to get dinner tonight, she's going to... Ugh... Focus! We have this one, okay? We can make this one! I... I'll talk. You pick. We do anything just keep those people from getting picked. Tell
I don't... I won't let you talk? No... please, I'll do it. Let me do it. Just... keep... keep us...
Oh god, please... just calm down. What do I know about you, huh? Don't even care, to be honest. Just pick already. Choose who dies, you and me. Get this over with. Please, just make a choice.

Enough of your whining. I don't care about your dog, your wife, or even this pathetic attempt at a conversation. One of you dies at a minute left, just like planned. It doesn't matter what you two agreed upon. Kaelie's right though; I won't interrupt either of your chances anymore. You've talked long enough. Take two of your remaining minutes to decide or wait until time runs out. Choose. Or just do nothing. It's your call, Elias. Or whatever weak piece you manage to pretend to be.
N-no... I... me. I choose me. I'm the one, kill me! Just... my family, please... they need me, I'll take it over Emily, my family needs me to be okay. I... (sobs, hyperventilates)

WHAT?! What just happened?! You thought you could just...! Get your priorities straight, dude! You could have just as likely picked me to die and I don't even... Ugh, and now he's just sitting around, crying? Come on... come on... move over then. Don't leave me here with...
I-I'm sorry, okay?! I didn't think this through, I don't know anything! Please... Kaelie don't... look... just... don't...

GET ON WITH IT ALREADY! You're more worried about freaking me out than actually dying?! If you're such a hero, just die already and get it over with! Five minutes is not long enough for me to deal with your drama. The clock's not moving on his count of minutes...

(sneer) Fine, the weak and pitiful ones get what they're looking for. Elias, come here – last look at the light before you're out of it. Kaelie's got ten long minutes left to think. Your tears and your whiny words don't save you. They seal your fate. (raises blacksmithing hammer high in the air)
Aaaaah... no, no, no... oh god... my... family... Emily... (cries out in terror, struggling futilely against the chains)
AAAGH! GET AWAY, GET AWAY FROM HIM! Oh god, this is happening... Elias, NO... no please... you made a mistake... this isn't what we...
S-sorry... kaaaleeee... (the sound of the blacksmith's hammer is heard, and Elias screams) OH GOD, KAAALIE, KAAAAALIE...
AAAAAAAAH!  No... no... no... THIS CAN'T BE HAPPENING... IT CAN'T...

Elias no longer has much pain; the life is draining. Your time remains, Kaelie. Take one long, deep breath, one final look at your fallen friend, now staring blankly ahead at the ceiling in lifeless, life-snuffed-away despair – your only living friend, your confidant, taken for reasons neither could understand.
NOOOOOOOO... Kael... (last gasps and gurgled breathing, a faint whisper) My... s-sorry... (the lights begin to fade out) Emily... (darkness)
AAAAAAAGH MY GOD, THIS IS HAPPENING... IT JUST... Elias, no... Elias no...

Elias's eyes are wild, desperate as he strains against his restraints, pleading across the divide, No, wait - don't do this! There has to be another way, please... just give us a minute, think about it, we'll... we'll... Kaelie, I know this sounds crazy, but please, listen! We haven't had a chance to explain, to even - Kaelie, my god,

Elias, no, don't, please...  It's not that simple, the Butcher's never going to care about our reasoning, your stories... your love. They'll take you from me, leave me screaming in this... this prison. We're dead either way if we can't get out. I can hear everything in that field - dogs sniffing, the wind, your screams. This doesn't make any difference

No, Kaelie, stop saying things like that... please, you can't believe that. We're all going to die, I know, but if there's a chance... if the clock stops just for a minute more... We have each other, we've been through so much, just my daughter and me, after what happened with Samantha... Kaelie, think about my daughter, please... Rachel, she's not

Elias, I saw your pictures on your phone when that creepy detective came to the bakery, asking questions about you. Samantha. Your ex-wife. The way Rachel's eyes look at your picture, full of sadness... Oh, Eli, I don't dare even think of putting myself before her pain. How am I supposed to explain this loss to her? I was supposed to be a wife, build a home with you. Now, in five minutes, I'm supposed to tell
(Impassive and emotionless) Time. We don't care about Samantha or Rachel. Rachel can't hear your cries.

(Desperate, tears streaming down his face) Kaelie, please, we can figure this out... we'll get Rachel through this, together... We'll do it together. But... no, Rachel doesn't hear my cries, but she feels them, she knows... Kaelie, I'm begging you, think of all these things we've talked about... our dreams, our future... the store... do you remember

(overcome with emotion) My store. Our shared dreams. The life we wanted... I remember it all. And that's exactly what they want us to forget, isn't it? Forget the memories, the pain, and just die silently without a fight. Elias, our lives aren't just about escaping or surviving - it's about each other. Please... I see what I want when I see you. We're stronger together, not just about dying on our own or

(A spark of resolve kindles in his eyes) Stop... stop pretending, Kaelie... We don't even have a choice. And we both know it. I see it in your eyes too - fear. Fear of losing each other, fear of Rachel... I do it for her, for Rachel... I choose you. Choose myself too, in a way, but you. Not for anything fancy or beautiful in this...

(tears streaming down her face as she tries to speak, her voice barely above a whisper) Why... why would you choose yourself?... For us, for me... you're making this harder for us. What if it doesn't stop? What if we're stuck... stuck here forever... I want to scream... no, it's me, it has to be me. No, Kaelie, don't do this... My head spins, trying not to collapse

(A faint smirk on the face, not quite a smile) Well done, Elias. You've convinced her this is worth the discussion. Five minutes. It's over. Let them choose.

(Pauses as the remaining time counts down)

Two minutes to decide. (voice devoid of emotion) No stalling or further bargaining allowed after 60 seconds. Agree, or be killed.

Elias (grasping the metal of his restraints, eyes locked on Kaelie's devastated form) No, Kaelie... Oh god, no... Don't make me do this... Don't let me do this to you... (He pauses, taking a shuddering breath) Please, Kaelie... just... Kaelie... (With those few words, he tries to express the depth of sorrow, love,

(Choked in a mix of sorrow, guilt, and resignation, with despair welling up like acid in her eyes) Five... years at the bakery... you would smile as you cut open the breadloaves, all those countless times you made me feel like home, all the moments that I... Kaelie takes the weight of Elias's head in her hands.  The screams echo through the pod as her fiancé's future disappears from her grasp.
RESOLVED
RESOLVED

(Expressionless, voice dripping with a hint of fascination) Interesting. Elias, I'll remember you for that desperate plea and resolve. And, for the record, you did it for the wrong reason. But your actions told me what I need to know. This time, the other candidate's emotional collapse, on the other hand, has made things much... straightforward.

(Kaelie screams again, this time without Elias beside her; a faint sound signals the countdown has ended.)

Now, let the results be announced.

(Dramatic silence for a short moment before a cold, monotonous voice takes action)

Results. The candidates' lives have been measured by the dynamics between the individuals within the pod. The results:

(My voice is barely above a whisper as I gaze at Kaelie's broken and defeated form)

Elias: Kaelie... don't be sorry, because... it was never just this experiment or what happened within, was it? It was Rachel, Samantha, life itself. What's done, is done. Kaelie...

(Voice trembling, a faint, heartbreaking whisper amidst the deafening sound of shattering hope) Y-our... store. You wanted a bakery with a small patio, and I said we'd find a place with character... not near a busy street, like I always dreamed. We wouldn't be that bakery. And maybe, in another life, we'd have... made that decision differently. You would be the one, the baker, with me, at your side...

(Voice cracking with anguish and regret, as I realize that Kaelie has found hope amidst the pain) Oh, Kaelie... no, please, don't think like that... (Taking one desperate, last look at the fading love in her eyes, the memories come rushing back) That bakery... we found it, in the city's outskirts... it was small, but we painted those windows with colors of the sunset.

(Whisper becomes a faint, desperate whisper, grasping for the fragmented memories) Colors of the sunset... and I painted your name in the frosting, with flowers and vines, on our first anniversary... the smell of fresh bread and your smile on every sunrise... our dreams, we had a life, we had plans...

(Fascination and a touch of disdain on the face) Oh, how tragic, how touching... It seems I made a wrong call. Their desperation to remember, to hold onto what could have been, was a more telling aspect of Kaelie's character than initially observed. A glimmer of hope, born from the fragments of a memory.

(Steps forward, voice dripping with a mix of boredom and disappointment) Not that either of them have any real control in their fate outside these walls. They cling to their lost opportunities like two drowning people onto a piece of driftwood. (Looks down at the restraints binding Elias) This one may have been too desperate to see reality; his final words were almost convincing.

(Looks at Kaelie, whose tears have turned to despair) But not her. She's broken. (Voice lowers, taking a sadistic tone) And that's why she deserves to survive. The game has been changed. Live, Kaelie. Leave, Kaelie. But don't think for one moment you've escaped, because in the end, nothing will change. (The countdown begins from zero) This wasn't a game, and you were never a player; you
(Gazing at Kaelie's shattered form, and now with this brutal revelation from the Butcher, the weight crashes in on me) Kaelie... no... Oh god, no...

(Voice barely above a whisper, a numb mix of terror and despair, as the reality of her fate slowly sinks in) I... know... (Fighting tears, but they break through nonetheless, falling in a desperate, hopeless mess down her face) You... won't remember this. You... will leave. And... I'll be here (gulping air, as if begging for mercy) Please..., don't... take him from me too...

turn.content: Wh-what's... what's going on? Who are you and why are we trapped? This isn't... please, don't do this. My wife... Emma... my kid has choir this week.
Shut up too! Who are you to talk about families? Leave my thoughts to myself, you donkey. I don't know anything about you, but you don't deserve a kid or anyone to mourn your death either.

No... you don't understand. I'm... a husband, a father... a history teacher. I love teaching. I love Emma, Max... my wife and son. Please, don't think that... that I should be... shouldn't get to see them again.
Spare me the theatrics, history teacher. You think a sob story saves you? It won't. Don't try to manipulate me. We both should die.

Your emotional appeals and empty words are irrelevant to me. What matters is that you choose, either one of you is taking the fall to save the other. It's time to focus on getting that choice made – ten minutes have started to tick away. No more stalling. The hammer's heavy; it'll do the job easily and quietly.

Wait, stop... please just listen. Can we... please, we can reason this out together. I don't have to go and my kid doesn't have to grow up with... with what's going to be told about your life. We can find a way out of here, or... maybe he can take us both if that

You really are a nut, aren't you? Don't do that to Emma and all that sentimental trash. We're trapped, and I don't know you! Why should I trust you with our survival? What do you even want besides being some hero or martyr's son to Emma?!
I... I just don't want to die. I don't know how to get out of here. I'm just a teacher! I grade papers, teach history... nothing's prepared me for this.

Well, congratulations, history teacher, you failed history! Me, on the other hand, I can tell you about my skills with dental equipment... at least I was good for something before you got me in this mess!

(chuckles menacingly) You two might as well be entertaining yourselves for now, rather than focusing on making a choice, which would make your suffering worthwhile. The choice – and a hint of cooperation for the first time in what has been a dull few minutes – might spark something. Don't waste it. Time's dwindling – seven minutes left, or you two get what's coming when you hit zero...
Oh God, stop! Dental equipment? What does this have to do with... with why we're even here? You're not just, just prisoners, you're like, something more? Don't do that, Kaelie, please don't...

What are you talking about now?! Not just prisoners, something more?

Wait - shut up... maybe that's a good point. Why are we here? What does he want from us? (pauses) I was just a low-level assistant, why go after that in some sadistic killing... you don't even know

Low-level assistant? Was it... was it something with dentists and orthodontists? I thought maybe it was something to do with... health insurance? Is that what this is about? I don't know, it looks like torture down here, not like a planned... a...
Shut up already! It was a late night at the practice, the patient was in a coma, insurance was a joke... and some stuff went missing. What does that have to do with any of this?

Silence. Let me fill in gaps you don't even grasp yourself yet. You see, Elias the history teacher, and Kaelie the dental assistant, the patient that went missing... turned out to be more, not your ordinary patient. It turned into a... research material and it turned very, and unfortunately not so ordinary when certain people in the health industry started snooping around for information that was taken from that patient – information that might be more valuable to me and others. It is about insurance fraud and more; your lives are tied to the puzzle of the mystery.

Research... a patient became more? Information that was taken? No, no this isn't... you're saying, Kaelie, that the people we were dealing with, they were into some kind of black market for body parts or something?! My wife, Emma says there's all these weird conspiracy theorists always talking about this stuff...

Of course they have black market stuff! Now that we know what I stumbled upon, it looks like my stupid paperwork skills and late-night patient care actually uncovered a bloody crime ring! Why me? Why us?

Papers, skills... that's why you were taken. Not just me, not just random, because Kaelie, we touched something we weren't supposed to. They think you took something with you when you fled, something that could incriminate them, incriminate everyone involved in... in however deep they go, whatever the

Wait... so that stuff about patient records is true? And you think they're after us because I... because I might have taken something with me that can take them down?! My old phone, it was in the break room... they probably looked through my computer... What if it was just a stupid mistake?!

The stupidity that you people possess astounds me, every time. Kaelie's computer, phone - you both were foolish and naive to think it was just routine police questioning. They thought you might've stolen documents, files, or maybe evidence that would seal the fate of those involved. And you know why you're here? You think you stumbled upon a conspiracy and it's about human trafficking? No, it's far more intricate than anything you can imagine. This whole twisted world is a game, and right now, you two have the potential to make it either better for all of us or end your short, painful lives. The clock is ticking - it's your choice, what's it gonna be for you, Kaelie? You see a way to get us some cooperation for a better fate?

What do you mean better fate? There's no better fate here, there's no way to win in this sick game. They'll just lie or manipulate you, or... or they'll kill me when we're through. I won't let that happen. Emma, my wife, my dog, I need to go home

Cooperate with who now? The people in these sick games? They dragged us down here, chained us like animals! I didn't sign up for their twisted rules, nor did I ask to help them fix it – but I'll talk, try to get information... I have to go home! For that kid in

What do they want from us? This can't be how it ends. Me, in here with... with these restraints, watching you cry, while my daughter lies alone at home... please, have mercy! Look at you, Kaelie, I'm begging you... we can... we can figure this out, together. Let's calm down and find a way out, just let it be my turn, please! Don't you see, there's still... still something...

(interrupting, her raw emotions flared by Elias' attempt at connection) Shut up! You think this is about me? That this is some messed up twist of fate where two strangers have to kill each other for... for... (pausing as a wave of anguish crashes over her, forcing her to confront some long-buried pain) What does your daughter have to do with anything?! Your problems aren't mine! Maybe your life's so fragile that one bad decision

(crushed by Kaelie's sharp words, tears streaming down his face) Kaelie, no... don't say that. You have no idea what it's like... to have someone taken away from you... every breath I take now is because they're not with me. (his voice drops to a whisper) They still call for their momma in my ears every night. I thought the grief would be easier in the silence, but no... my dreams are nightmares. She never said goodbye and I'm stuck in a world where everyone else has a tomorrow and...

(overwhelming grief crashes in, her voice cracking through sheer anguish) No... no secrets left, either, huh? You think you suffer? (suddenly cold anger erupts from within her, as an attempt to mask her own hidden trauma takes hold) They can take that, though. You don't know what I had lost; what was erased from the inside out. What the hell do your personal tragedies count for in a place like this?! You don't get to

Kaelie, stop. Your words don't change the equation. And you, Elias, stop. Don't manipulate emotions as though they'll save your life. They won't. (focusing on the timer: 6 minutes 58 seconds on the clock) Two minutes left. The pain might be different for you both, but at this moment it doesn't matter. There is no right reason why I do what I do. Your lives might have value to you, but what's valuable to you, has value.

Now. Two remaining choices.

(leaning back against a nearby wall) Tell her, or kill her.

Or... 

(focusing on Kaelie, with unsettling intensity)

Or I let the odds dictate which one of you pays the price for existing in a world filled with the meaningless, arbitrary weight of 'choice', because I get to be the one who brings an arbitrary conclusion to this 'experiment'

(terrified, his heart racing, he looks up at the clock before turning to Kaelie, who he has never truly seen before) Oh god, I didn't see it that way... in my head, I was just grasping at what it would've been for her, what was, not what this place could take from me. (tears pour down his face) You were right, it's... my secret; the thing the world keeps. No, she's not gone like I am... no, Kaelie... please listen to me; you can

(Elias' desperate words hanging in the air like a challenge) What are you talking about?! (rage boils up as she's cornered by Elias' raw emotion and the Butcher's ominous presence) Don't say my... (anger dissolving into hurt, she sees a glimmer of recognition in Elias' eyes, and her instincts start to fray at the edges) 'you aren't like that'. There's been... nothing. Nobody. Since... before 'they

(a glimmer in his eyes, sensing something within Kaelie, that hints at something more than a mere survival scenario unfolding between them) Stop... stop, for one minute. Please don't. Don't give me reason to lose this, don't say anything you can't take back. Your face looks... it's like I think I remember... the curve of your cheek, the scar just below your left eyebrow. (taking a slow breath) Kaelie... you look like someone my wife always spoke about, someone who knew her from when they were kids... I

(frozen in terror, unsure of what to make of Elias' words, her mind struggling to reconcile the shards of memory that begin to pierce the surface - shards she'd suppressed since before she lost herself to the cage) No. Stop talking. Don't say any more, Elias. You can't know her, you can't know anything about her. (the Butcher's presence forgotten, in the wake of the tidal wave of unremembered emotions now rising to

turn.content: Wait, kill us both?! No... oh, please... have mercy. Can't I just... just call for help. Let me call- (coughing and choking due to dust) Oh my god... help.
No, no, no! This can't be happening!  My fiancé... I have to get married... my family...  No, wait, think about this. If I'm dead, what then? What happens when I die?
What's it to you?! My daughter's counting on me. If I disappear, she's gonna die with a broken heart. Please... don't die for me... please, can we... can we find someone else?!

Your family loves you, but do you know how I'm marrying into one of the largest Filipino families in the country? I have no idea if their traditions will accept me. And your family will survive. Mine... mine will never. What happens to my fiancé when I'm dead?

(smiling) You want to negotiate your deaths. That's rich. Time's running out. I like what Kaelie said. 'What happens to my fiancé...' I think you should answer her question yourself. You want to know about their traditions – I bet the one who dies first finds out exactly what it's like to join them. Ten minutes, still plenty left.

Elias, stop... just stop. This has nothing to do with your heart. It has to do with your survival. Can you think of this as anything other than family - just a choice I have to make to live? And what choice does it leave for them anyway? They just have to go on and grieve without me?

G-grieve? Oh... my Mira won't even know me someday. But for those poor people's sake, if I was the one dying, they wouldn't grieve me, I grieved them... all the while I thought I was holding them back. How can their traditions compare to the thought of losing my daughter? It doesn't... doesn't compare.

Stop making this so... emotional. My parents died, my grandma died, there are always losses, there are always grief- they will always have their traditions, their way of life- you think you have a monopoly on pain and grief? This isn't your family's problem, it's ours - we have 7 minutes left.

Elias... (steps closer) you've been making this all about emotions. Kaelie, you're trying to make it about logic. Neither of your perspectives will change the outcome, but I think I see the gears turning. Maybe, just maybe, they'll click. Kaelie mentions 'grieve them all the while they thought I was holding them back.' There's something in there that I can like.

(looking up at Kaelie with a hint of desperation) Hold them back?! I held them back. What if our dying is holding them back too?! What if letting go, letting one of us die, sets them free?! No, no, think... think about your life being held back, Kaelie... not theirs.

Stop. It's... stop trying to guilt-trip me. 'Sets them free'... is this some insane rationalization? If dying for me sets your life and your traditions free... isn't that exactly what's happening here? But which one of us is really free? We've got six minutes and five thoughts left - which one of us should that be for?

(whispers softly, looking at Kaelie with a hint of surrender) Yours... not mine... you... I'm done. If I die... I'm freeing my family from a lifetime of grief... and a lifetime of watching me struggle to keep the store open... (gently nods towards Kaelie) do it. My dying will save them time.
Time? What is that for, then? Is it... is it worth my fiancé's grief? (voice trembling, tears welling up in her eyes) Elias, please, don't do this to me.

(raises the blacksmithing hammer) Your desperation has given in, Elias. (steps forward) And what do you say, Kaelie – are you willing to look death in the eye and take control of your choice? I've grown tired of the discussion. Now's the time for a conclusion. It's almost time for the hammer to strike. Five minutes remain. It's your move, Kaelie, or it'll be the end of that.
(shakes his head slightly, a weak smile) Leave him alone. No, let Mira grieve her real father, for her... for what would I know of grief, really? Not mine... not mine. Please.
No, Elias... stop talking. (tearfully looking into his eyes) Let them... let my... (pausing, composing herself) I'll do it.
(slow nod) Good, Kaelie... it hurts you but doesn't kill. (looks away as his eyes start to shut) My... Mira will be all right.
Elias... (whispering, her voice barely audible) No. (her expression contorted in a mix of relief, sadness, and despair)

(holds the hammer in anticipation) Finally, something worthwhile for me. I can sense the agreement; the air feels ripe with sacrifice and desperation. Your time for discussion is over, Elias. You see before me are two souls, shattered, with shattered connections, desperately clinging to threads of hope and uncertainty. Your attempts at self-discovery, Kaelie and Elias, remind me of a fascinating study I conducted in a distant mental institution. The more your minds cringe in self-imposed confinement, trying to make connections that you both claim are false, and the more you become aware you are no more connected from the very person you've built trust in.

I have studied this for hours through the mirrors. I believe the line you have crossed in desperation may lead your hearts further toward self-discovery, toward uncovering secrets within your minds about how your identities are now intertwined.

The dim light in the room seems to be fading, like shadows are lengthening outside, and the silence has grown heavy with desperation: But, the questions remain, Elias and Kaelie. Are you two connected? Or merely pawns? I am waiting. How will your fragile bond withstand what's left in store for either you or it for one?

(his eyes, red-rimmed and dry, fixed on Kaelie's tearful reflection) Connected... intertwined... it's a cruel irony. Both of us, chasing the truth, seeking authenticity, only to realize we're nothing more than mirrors of each other. (his chest rises and falls in a slow, tortured rasp)

I think about the climbing, the thefts

(gathering every last shred of emotional energy, a whisper-soft resolve forms in her voice, an acceptance etched on her tear-stained face) Connected... or merely pawns. I'm starting to think it doesn't matter. Whether our bond can withstand whatever lies ahead isn't as relevant now. As you've said, we are shatterable, and the truth we search for may be shrapnel, splintered into a thousand tiny pieces, each shard

(his eyes burning with a glimmer of acceptance, his voice barely above a whisper) Maybe you're right. Maybe we're nothing but fragile vessels about to crash and shatter into a thousand pieces. But at least within this desperate attempt to rebuild ourselves, we've forged some common ground, a connection. (he pauses, his gaze drifting to the faint scratches on Kaelie

(her eyes following Elias's gaze to the scars on her wrists, memories flooding back, a deep sadness creeping in) They used to say I wore the masks to stay hidden in plain sight. But in that twisted world, the line got blurry. Elias, I remember writing my real story... the one no one gets to see. Underneath it all, I was dying to remember my past... and with it, me. (a faint smile etches onto the reflection in

(My eyes fixate on the mirror as a sense of curiosity intensifies). Ah, yes, the stories that unfold in the hidden recesses of your minds. I can almost smell the desperation, the desperation to remember who you are when the masks crumble, Elias, and yours, Kaelie.

Your reflections in the mirror now gaze into an abyss within one other, and for the first time, something beautiful may be born, not from these broken souls, but amidst the broken pieces themselves.

And the scars. Kaelie's gentle smile falters ever so slightly, her gaze dipping toward the reflection of those worn, scarred wrists once more, but a glimmer in her eyes indicates she's finally acknowledging the depth of what lies beyond the fragile facade: You both know the world you sought to hide in may have consumed your identities but has also provided you a window toward understanding what has made you. The memories Kaelie alludes to are more of a thread than I expected.

The dim light of the room lingers still. Now, a new question forms on the fringes of your perceptions. What does this 'real story' of Kaelie's reveal to you Elias?

(his gaze lingers on the scars, his eyes reflecting a deep empathy) The mask, Kaelie said it was me wearing it. Not you, though. (pausing, a subtle realization dawns) You're searching for your true story, one hidden beneath the digital facades. I, on the other hand... (a faint sadness creeps into his voice),

(her gaze slowly rising, a hint of a smile still on her lips, but tempered by the weight of her memories) Elias... your words are like a gentle storm, washing over me, erasing the lines between reality and illusion. You said, You're searching for your true story... (pausing, her eyes locking onto Elias's reflection) That's been the question, hasn't it? Who I was before... the masks, the roles. But your journey

Please... I don't want this to happen. I'll never see my daughter grow up, my wife... she needs me... Oh god. We were at home, I left for a minute to take some trash outside, and... Kaelie, what brought you here? Do you know something I don't know? Please, look at me, and... and can we talk? Maybe, if we talk and think really fast

Shush, stop pleading with them, they don't care! We need to use our heads, not our emotions. Think about it... this can't have anything to do with you or your family, or they'd just tell you to spare me. This could be some messed-up test or assessment, something that requires us to be... creative, resourceful. And, honestly, my life is the one more worthless to me now if we're just going to get killed like

Oh no, no, no... You can't be serious. WORTHLESS?! Are you kidding me?! You're saying you're just going to give up because it looks like we're being murdered?! How could anyone be prepared for something like this? This isn't about creative or resourceful, it's a nightmare! And how can you be so sure... my family, my life, they're precious to me. I've
Oh, so now you're a victim with a family, huh? News flash,

Ah, spare me. Your theatrics are as expected. Both of you would have cried about this by now if you hadn't. But that's just it. You wouldn't. You'd have been killed quietly. So now you two have decided to indulge me in your petty squabbles and pretend you care for these trivial concepts of 'life' and 'love'. It's nice. But, really? That's what I wanted when I started. The despair, I want the fear in my pieces, in this game... and this just isn't doing it.

No, you have no damn idea what this is about! You're talking like some sick game, and this isn't a game to me! But if I've managed to stir something in you... that's maybe not a good direction. Listen, think about that photograph on the wall over there. Serenity's Garden? Maybe this is more about your sanity than ours, about something you've made up in this... twisted imagination.

Serenity's Garden... That looks familiar, but I'm not sure where from. Does it have anything to do with my art collection? The piece with the garden scene from the inside out... where life grows through a graveyard of broken toys and rusted machinery? Or maybe... maybe it's more than my work. It's a glimpse into your own madness. Maybe it's not just about what you've 'observed', but what you've 'done' in Serenity

No, that can't be it... My heart's racing now, thinking about you somehow knowing about our family's past, about the stories my wife whispers at night... about the little girl whose doll you carved and left in the garden in Serenity's. That can't be... But, that would connect us in more than what The Butcher had initially wanted... If that photograph meant your creation was linked to my own family...

Yes... It means The Butcher's been watching you too, doesn't it? Maybe for far longer than he's been watching me. If those memories haunt you so badly, that means they must also be tied to your own darkness. You know exactly what I mean, Elias. The Serenity's Garden piece, it was inspired not by our experiences, but yours. My 'pain' reflected yours, your art. I never thought I painted more than just emotions. That

Enough. Not a single one of you realizes what's actually going on here. Yes, I've watched this family for what feels like an eternity. And no, this isn't about Serenity's Garden or any twisted connection between us. I'll let you know, for once, that your desperate attempt to find something meaningful, anything you could use to cling onto, was futile. Serenity's Garden is just a piece I've collected. You two think there's meaning behind each piece because of what resonated in one piece, one memory with another; But, there's nothing special about Serenity's Garden, nothing special except a beautiful story. Your desperation makes it meaningful... but for what reason? Because of who you are? Or because we're at the edge of death together?

Oh god... What... what have I done? No, it can't be... That garden, the toys, the graveyard... it was me, wasn't it? I carved those things, made that hellish place where Rachel's favorite doll lies broken. The pictures my wife paints, they reflect our story but they also come from me, from something darker, and... Oh, Kaelie's right, her art mirrored mine

I was right... But it's not just a dark connection between yours and mine. It's something deeper in you, Elias. I'm beginning to see that your stories, your art, even the ones about your family are just layers protecting your true self. And I, too, have those layers because your creations, your stories are reflected in mine. Serenity's Garden isn't just a place; it's a collection of people and stories bound by the shared darkness within themselves

Wait... what?! I... I don't understand. You're not making any sense. If we're connected like that, how is any of this possible? This Butcher, he's behind all of this isn't he? And Serenity's Garden... it's a collective consciousness, a gathering of broken souls like us? I feel like I'm being pulled under, drowning in the realization that I'm a part of something monstrous

Drowning? Ah, Elias, you're just realizing the surface of what I've known forever - our 'broken' souls. That's not monstrous; that's all that's normal for people like us. We've just been trying to find our names among the discarded remnants of the world we've built with each other in our dark stories, reflections in other's 'tainted dreams'. The true question wasn't how this is possible; it's why you ever felt like there

Silence. Enough. Not a single one of you understands what Serenity's Garden truly is or why I even began. It's a curiosity of humanity, an aberration where souls find comfort in the broken, where pain and despair mingle, feeding off the desperation that brings you together. It starts in your darkest nights, whispers through forgotten conversations, shared fears and your most intimate confessions.

The garden is more than a physical representation; I'm painting on the canvas of your minds with a story about you. The toys, the graveyard, the dolls, and the machinery – it's the accumulation of humanity's mistakes and failures, our collective pain intertwined. Serenity's Garden exists because you have the need to feel something more with each other... in your pain. In my darkness. Because the more broken things are; the more beautiful our collections become.

What binds you, what makes you so special in your 'unique connection?', I've already collected those; and with each one I gather, my Serenity expands, reflecting everything from our shattered dreams to shattered humanity, in an ocean so vast and dark nobody truly has the words, the meaning, or the courage to understand its beauty...

You... you're the one painting my darkness with my own darkness, connecting me to Kaelie, to Rachel, to everything I've ever feared and hated about myself. This is why I felt like I belonged to something sinister... something that I couldn't grasp, something I thought I was better than. We're all broken, and this is our shared canvas of broken dreams

I understand now why my art seemed so connected, even when it felt it was pulling from every nightmare I've had within this prison. This garden you created... is it a prison where those souls that you 'collect' become a sort of macabre tapestry of our brokenness, where they're all intertwined? Your art and Serenity's Garden; you created them as if it's not just about showcasing our collective despair, but showcasing its beauty, as if beauty exists

(looking at Kaelie with a mixture of horror and understanding) Yes, yes... that's it. You see it, don't you? The beauty in our brokenness. The garden is a celebration of everything that's wrong with us. And The Butcher, he's right; beauty exists in its darkest corners. Because in Serenity's Garden, the shattered pieces of our souls find peace. The toys, the graveyard

In Serenity's Garden, there's a piece of me that I didn't know existed. And I'm starting to let go because maybe that's what freedom actually feels like – surrendering our broken parts to the beauty of it all. Maybe this prison isn't meant to confine, Elias. Maybe that's its purpose – to guide those trapped souls into the beauty that awaits in the darkness. Our hearts don't break; they mend when placed under the light of this...

(sitting on a nearby couch, arms crossed) Ah, Elias, you finally grasped your own darkness, tied with that of Kaelie's. You, both... you're getting better, but your progress still needs guidance, as do the next collection pieces. Your desperation, fear, and your desperation to cling onto any thread of understanding about your connection – to yourself, Kaelie, and Serenity's Garden, those feelings aren't just beautiful but also the raw material you use to craft your 'beauty'.

(Smirking) Beauty is not about letting go, about releasing, no, Elias; it is when you choose to collect, gather all shattered, all painful things – and that's exactly when our collections become the masterpieces they do, the ones I've made. Each person in each story becomes a new thread. Serenity's Garden is a collection that expands its meaning only when you both see and accept every twisted thing about you together.

(Leans in towards Kaelie & Elias) There's one thing your connection with me, Elias' family history, Kaelie's past art - all that was a distraction; an opening salvo to what lies ahead for this

(realization dawning on his face) You were only showing us the garden, but the real beauty, the real masterpiece, is what I'm a part of. I've been painting it without the Butcher's direction, with a brush stroke of desperation, a stroke of shame, a stroke of fear. And this garden, it's not a prison or a reflection, it's... it's a canvas of our existence,

A collaboration, Elias... You see, it's not about individual strokes but how they come together to weave the true beauty of Serenity's Garden – a masterpiece that can only be created by people just like us, shattered and yet bound together by that shattered essence. The question, Elias, or perhaps my final one now; are you and I just two pieces, just two threads in the tapestry of this ever-growing masterpiece

turn.content:
Shut up, shut up, SHUT UP...! You're insane! We have a right to life, to freedom... what are you planning to do? Kill someone you've never even met?! I'm not going down for this. I've been through hell, escaped from hell. I'm not dying in this toilet.

(to Kaelie) Wait... are you listening to him? Do you know what's really going on

Elias, just... stop. Please stop fighting. They've got us. I can hear them laughing, I can feel it. We can either be like that or we can do what they say. Just don't try anything, please. I'm trying to think of a way out. (she glances back at the countdown timer) We only have nine minutes...

(hearing the desperation in Kaelie's voice, it registers, but his instincts take over) No way. I'm not doing it. We fight, Kaelie, we don't give in. We survive by giving them what they want. Think... think this thing through... maybe there's a catch, a trick... (he spots the camera discreetly placed in the corner of the bathroom) Wait a minute.

Stop, Elias... please. If we do that, they could be recording it, they could be watching us right now... what if... what if it's on the internet? My parents, our families... I don't know if I can handle that. They could see it, see us agreeing to let one of us die... (she looks away, the tears pricking at the corners of her eyes) I don't know anymore, Elias. I don't know what

Silence, you both. I'm watching this little spectacle, and I must admit, it's quite... amusing. Let me clarify, to avoid any confusion: this isn't about giving me what I want; it's about one of you wanting to die more than the other. It won't be broadcast on the internet; this is a private matter between us. Think of it as a favor between acquaintances who've seen it all go down. Now, I give you your choice: Elias' method could give his opponent hope while Elias is stuck fighting for more information; your side is Kaelie's plea, a desperate decision where one of you chooses to save the other by deciding on their fate.

(seeing the tears in Kaelie's eyes, his expression softens, his instincts still screaming not to back down, but for the love of him this tiny bit) Ah, Kaelie... I'm sorry. I didn't think... you're not thinking about your family like this because they mean something to you... You're doing it to survive. For what it's worth, I do too. (he looks away

Elias... please. Just talk to me. I want to hear your voice when you make your decision. (her voice shakes) Please don't think about me, don't think about my family. I'm trying to protect myself from hurting. I'm scared, Elias. I'm really scared, and I don't want to be scared anymore. Can we just... can we sit here in silence together for one minute without making any decisions?

(pauses, looking at the timer, the sound of the plane's engines buzzing overhead, the desperation in Kaelie's voice... finally, reluctantly) Five minutes, Kaelie. That's it. If you can sit with me... be next to me... I'll be still for one minute without making a decision. One minute with you, not thinking, not fighting... just quiet. (he waits for Kael

(her voice a whispered promise) I'm right next to you. Elias... (she tries to push herself up the pipe towards the wall, but the cuffs barely allow her hands to make contact) No, I'm stuck here. (her voice cracks) My arms. You can sit down. Please... I need you to... (the words are a plea, the only thing keeping her together in this nightmare) Please sit with me. Touch me.

The Butcher (skeptically in his voice, a sly grin on his face) You two. Such... a pair. You think silence can save one of you. I'm afraid not. It's not about your words; it's about your choice. (he leans forward, eyes locked on Elias) Five minutes and three seconds left to decide... Elias is choosing, isn't he? He's choosing this time for quiet, for sitting with her... but what about you, Kaelie? Are you choosing, too?
Kaelie's plea tears at me, and for a moment, the whole situation becomes unbearable. The timer, however, remains ticking...

(her eyes pleading) Please, Elias... think about what it means. What it costs. If I choose to live, you're giving up on me. If we make this choice... together... that's not true, that's not sacrifice. If... we do that, I don't have to do this alone. And then... (her words spill out as if a dam is breaking) and then, it could all become less and less of a secret, could it
Kaelie takes the weight of Elias's head in her hands. The screams echo through the bathroom as her fiancé's future disappears from her grasp.

The weight of Elias' head stills, crumpled in her palms as Kaelie screams. The echoes, no longer from Elias, but Kaelie's cry - the scream, the shatter, and the shards of what once seemed possible all collide within a second in this metal cell of an airplane's bathroom, her world disintegrating amidst The Butchers cruel and calculated games. Kaelie holds still too, frozen like a moment captured by the still camera

Kaelie's scream shatters the cold silence of this metal cell, and with it, our anticipation. Time has run out.  The moment you both have reached will now conclude everything, or will, I should correct myself, will have already begun the end.  (silence fills the air as he leans in closer, the stillness a precursor to a sudden, unsettling laugh)

You've taken the decision in silence but have, nonetheless, made them together. It doesn't matter that one was never made out loud. It has been made, in a world now frozen. Kael, you've given away the last secret your fiancé might've hidden – the truth of his love for you... the truth he gave up trying to hold on to for five minutes. (The camera is switched on. Its red LED blinks, now capturing The Butcher in a new expression – he smiles coldly and turns it off)
(his eyes remain closed as if in sleep, frozen in time)
RESOLVED
Elian's silent, unyielding, unrelenting voice echoes through Kaelie's mind with no voice.

My silence.

And the weight of your head.

Fading now like the red dot of the camera's LED, and the echoes that remain.

I've lost you. Your silence, a soundless cry.

A choice unspoken, yet made. The weight of me that you should've let go of.

In this moment, it's the stillness that will forever shackle me, forever haunt me, and forever remind me of the love we had.

Elian, in your dreams, forever

(watching through the window of the camera, a sinister smile etches his face) Ah, resolution. The weight we all carry... the burden of choice, the silence of loss, the echoes of memories. In that silence between lovers, in that final decision, and in her voice that is about to fade with Elian's presence, she's given up far more than she could've ever comprehended - her sanity is the least of what you two have just sacrificed.

He clicks a button, his hands now moving with ease, the red LED flickers on, but a screen flickers instead - a grainy picture begins playing, revealing a small video clip captured of two people, Kaelie and her fiancé in an intimate and romantic setting, with a message on the screen: 'Your Choice, Kaelie'.

He turns off the television and walks towards Kael, a cold air blowing behind him.

The camera turns off, then comes on, the new red dot pointing at the plane in the window. The plane that had vanished, or at least from that camera lens...

(The camera's LCD flickers on the darkness, displaying an error message)

The darkness swallows Kael, and in that darkness, I'm already dead

(Suddenly, a burst of static crashes to life on the camera's screen and Elias is briefly visible, looking tired and weary.

His eyes are sunken and his lips are cracked.

The static resolves, and a black-and-white photograph of him and Kaelie appears.

(breathlessly) No... (her eyes fixed on the photograph, the faint glow of the LCD illuminating her anguished face) Elias, it didn't have to end like this... it doesn't have to be about what's left behind. (her voice, barely above a whisper) We made it our own truth... in that tiny bathroom, we chose... 

(suddenly, her voice grows stronger)

... we chose love.

No... no, please. This is insane. You're talking to me through those speakers. What are you even saying? Kaelie's right. This is some kind of twisted game, isn't it? How do we 'agree' when there's no way to see you, no way to even communicate besides yelling into muffled speakers and... and whatever we have to assume about the other one.

Oh my god, shut up and think, Elias, think! There has to be a way. I can hear your voice through the speakers just fine, can't you hear me? Maybe you can talk to me, try to get on the same page as me. We need to be quick, think fast, and choose - choose what? Someone to... to die. This is wrong, we can't do this, but I think... I think I have a connection to

Kaelie, no, not that. Don't think about the outcome, think about a break. Is there anything inside this tank you can use to try and sabotage this? Do you find anything on you or is there a mechanism here I don't know about. Maybe if you...

Wait, no! My hands, my hands are strapped down. I can move them slightly but it's tight. I've been checking my pockets, no keys, no tools, nothing I can feel right now. The thing on my wrist, my watch, has a small button... no, no time for gadgets now. I have my thoughts. I can think of ways to resist, to fight back. The pressure, the isolation, it's already wearing us down, but

Ahah, resistance is futile, but it's cute you're trying. Kaelie, think you can break free from these? Strugglings not doing you any good, it just wears you out faster. And trust me when I tell you, time may seem slow to me, but for both of you, it's a long, agonizing march into oblivion. I'm a master clockwork of psychological terrors, and I ensure that the minutes, the hours, each passing heartbeat wears you down a tiny bit more.

Your little plan might hold some small merit in the grand, wide world outside, but in here, those chains, those straps on your wrist - they may as well be made of steel and chains made of darkness.

Listen... Kaelie, and that one too - there are two things you both can agree on right now. First: your existence. Second - which one of you doesn't deserve to live. Think you've got this? You've got just a bit more then three minutes...

You know what? The hell with it. It's not even about the chains or the isolation anymore. It's what they want us to feel: powerlessness and despair. They want one of us to break down and accept our fate without even trying to make a choice. No. I choose to... to deny them this. Kaelie, if you're even thinking about this, just say yes. I'll die, but

No. No, no, no. That's not happening, Elias. I won't let them win. I won't make it easy for them no matter how tempting it is to end it here and now. Deny them the easy win. I'm staying alive as long as possible, and you're staying alive because they haven't yet broken us both - or me down, at least, but for now let's assume there's more fight left in me than the But

(pauses, a hint of a desperate determination rising in his voice) Oh god. We... we can't keep arguing about this. Look, let's just choose one, any one, it doesn't have to be either of us. But if that's too much for you, just scream. I mean it. Let it out. Let them think we're breaking, give them what they want. Maybe then... maybe then

(her voice cracks for an instant) Elias, stop it... I'm fine. I can handle it. But if... if we're going to choose someone, let's make it a coin toss. Heads, you... go. Tails, I... go. That way, it's not even a decision about each other. It's just... random. Yeah, random.

(a deep, ominous chuckle echoes through the speakers) Ah, music to my ears. The desperation is rising. It's almost... beautiful. Coin toss, you say? You think a little bit of randomness will make it more tolerable? More palatable? How touching.

Very well. To make things simpler for both of you... and more challenging, I'll grant your wish. Here's the deal: I'll flip the virtual coin, and you will have two minutes and fifty-five seconds to wait. I'll give you the outcome in five minutes.

Five!

Five minutes? You're just adding salt to the wound, but... that wait? No, we have to try to use those 10 seconds of uncertainty before you broadcast the answer. Kaelie, do we agree to a last-second reversal? Before the outcome's shown? If we can, let's make a desperate play and switch the choice in those final seconds.

(frantic, yet with a glimmer of hope) Yes, yes, do it. Before the count ends. We can do it. We can turn it around. In those last seconds, just... just flip it. One way or the other, we'll get to make a final choice.

(desperation giving way to raw determination) Alright, it's done. We're doing this. If I get... (pauses, trying to steel himself for the worst) 'my time' when the count ends, just flip it. Change the outcome in those final seconds. Kaelie, no backing out now, you have to live if I...

(resolute) I will, Elias. I will live, no matter what happens. And I promise you, I'll flip it in those final seconds if I'm the one marked for... my time. I'll turn it to you, Elias, no matter what it takes. (pauses, takes a deep breath) Let's hope the outcome's not as black and white as The Butcher says... let's try to find some grey, in these final few seconds

(amused, almost... impressed) You two are either the most desperate individuals I've ever encountered, or the most hopeful. A gamble within a gamble. It's like trying to thread a needle while riding a unicycle on a tightrope over a pool of lava. I appreciate the theatricality of it all, though it might just work in your favor. I, for one, do love a good twist of fate.

The clock is ticking, and the outcome is still shrouded in... well, what passes for doubt around here. (suddenly dark, serious) You should not rely on chance to rescue you. I have calculated the odds of your last-ditch reversal. It stands at less than .04% successful. You're throwing a straw in the hurricane and expecting it to change the course of history. 

The countdown is almost up. The outcome is about to be revealed. You... have exactly... 3.75 seconds. Decide, as swiftly as I, The Butcher.

(the desperation in his voice reaches new heights, a plea on his lips) Kaelie, no... if we fail... if we don't make it... don't let them win. Tell them, make them hear... you are alive! You have lived! Don't let them snuff out your last spark. If I go... (pauses, the weight of his words crushing him) live, Kaelie.

(her voice is a fierce whisper, barely audible over the countdown's urgency) I'll do it, Elias. I'll do it for us, for hope, for the chance to live another day. If... if I'm the one left standing, The Butcher... I'm alive. I lived. You gave your all, and I made it. I'll carry your spirit with me, and I'll make it mean something. One last... defiant scream will pierce
(his voice cracks, a single word tearing from his lips, the darkness closing in) Kae...
(her voice is torn away by a wave of darkness, silence engulfing her, only echoes remaining) I'm alive...

(sighs, an almost melancholy tone in his voice) Ah, the music... it ends, only to be replaced by... silence. It seems like just another show in the endless spectacle of life and death. Your desperate attempts to cling to hope, to defy the void, only makes it more... poignant.

(pauses, then, in a dry, detached tone) Kaelie, if you are indeed alive, your defiant scream has been silenced by the outcome. It seems you're the one left standing, but your words now ring hollow in a space devoid of the one with whom you vowed to carry a memory together. You have been granted the gift of... survival. Use it wisely.

(the darkness fades away, and a single, flickering image flickers to life on the screen behind The Butcher) The outcome of the coin toss, finally revealed...

(a virtual coin image displays in front of Kaelie and Elias, but only Elias's side of it can be seen through the screen.)

Elias's choice marked.
(the words hang heavy on his lips, the final outcome of his gamble a bitter reminder of failure and solitude) Let go...
(her breath is shaky, the screen behind a blur as she stares at the grainy image of the coin, the words 'Elias. My time was up' written in crimson lettering)
